step_by_step_reasoning: Based on the whiteboard diagram and transcript, there are three main EC2-based components (Web Server/Job Manager, G3 GPU instance with Asset Manager, and IMF Wrapper), a Database, S3, a Lambda function, and a Web Browser actor. The workflow consists of three distinct flows: 1) metadata extraction and display, 2) typical GPU retiming/processing, and 3) IMF-wrapped video processing.

graph.name: Cinefilm PixelStrings Video Processing Platform
graph.notes: PixelStrings runs video processing (retiming, deinterlacing, noise removal) on GPU-enabled G3 EC2 instances, leveraging AWS Lambda to extract metadata and write to S3.

nodes.name: Web Browser
nodes.notes: User accesses the PixelStrings platform via Chrome.

nodes.name: Web Server & Job Manager
nodes.notes: Handles user login, database queries, and parallel job orchestration.

nodes.name: Database
nodes.notes: Caches metadata. The web server reads from it to update the client UI.

nodes.name: S3 Bucket
nodes.notes: User-managed bucket storing input and output video clips.

nodes.name: Metadata Lambda
nodes.notes: Runs MediaInfo to fetch metadata from video clips in S3.

nodes.name: G3 GPU Instance (Asset Manager)
nodes.notes: Runs GPU-accelerated workloads (Wormhole, Spacetime, Dark Energy).

nodes.name: IMF Wrapping Server
nodes.notes: Formats finished video clips into Netflix-compatible Interoperable Master Format.

edges.notes: User logs in and inputs S3 credentials.
Job Manager invokes Lambda to get video metadata.
Lambda queries video files in S3.
Job Manager caches metadata in the database.
Web server retrieves cached metadata to show in browser.
Job Manager tells Asset Manager on G3 to fetch the input clip.
Asset Manager copies clip from S3 to local G3 storage.
Asset Manager copies processed output clip back to S3.
Job completed status updated on UI.
Asset Manager fetches clip from S3.
Job Manager routes completed GPU output directly to IMF server.
IMF wrap output copied back to customer S3 bucket.
Web UI displays job complete status.
